file.content:
"blur on, ignore_alpha 1, match:namespace waybar"
        "blur on, ignore_alpha 1, match:namespace notifications"
      ];

      # --- Window Rules ---
      # Hide Chrome's Spotify "now playing" floating popup (redundant with waybar mpris)
      windowrule = [
        "match:class ^(google-chrome)$, match:float yes, match:title .*•.*, workspace special:trash silent"
        "match:class ^(google-chrome)$, match:float yes, match:title .*is sharing your screen.*, workspace special:trash silent"
      ];

      # --- Keybindings ---
      # NOTE: Keyboard is a Kinesis Advantage (split ergo). Super, Enter, Space,
      # Ctrl(R), PgUp, PgDn are all on the right thumb cluster. Avoid chords
      # combining any two of these keys (e.g. Super+Return, Super+Space).
      # Super+Backspace works (Backspace is on the left thumb cluster).
      #
      # Directional home row keys: J=left, K=down, L=up, ;=right
      # (Kinesis Advantage key wells make JKL; more natural than HJKL)
      #

content.thinking:
The fix is straightforward. Line 183 needs the class matcher removed. The waybar screenshare indicator at line 264 uses `hyprctl clients -j` to detect windows with "is sharing your screen" in the title - this should still work since `hyprctl clients` lists all windows including those on special workspaces.

Let me make the fix.

content.text: The issue is clear. Line 183 matches `class ^(google-chrome)$` but the overlay has an empty class. The waybar indicator (line 264) just checks the title via `hyprctl clients -j`, which lists windows on all workspaces including special ones, so it will keep working.

input.new_string: "match:float yes, match:title .*is sharing your screen.*, workspace special:trash silent"

content.content:
=== Pre-PR Review ===

Peer Review (haiku):
Error: running peer review: failed to parse review response: invalid character 'I' looking for beginning of value; response text: "I need to see the diff content to review it. Let me examine the provided diff carefully.

Looking at this NixOS configuration diff, here are the issues I've found:

```json
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "file": "hosts/classic-laddie/default.nix",
      "line": 364,
      "description": "Bind mount target path `/run/agenix-klaus/github-token` won't exist before the mount attempt. The tmpfiles rule creates the parent directory but not the file/directory needed as the mount point. Bind mounts require the target path to exist. Either create the target with tmpfiles (e.g., `f /run/agenix-klaus/github-token 0400 root root`) or use a different approach."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "file": "hosts/classic-laddie/default.nix",
      "line": 362,
      "description": "The systemd.mounts unit lacks an explicit dependency on agenix secret activation. While secrets are typically created during system activation (before systemd services start), the missing explicit ordering makes this brittle. Consider adding documentation or an explicit ordering dependency if NixOS provides it."
    },
    {
      "severity": "low",
      "file": "hosts/classic-laddie/default.nix",
      "line": 385,
      "description": "Formatting inconsistency: the new tmpfiles rule comment lacks a trailing comment line unlike the existing rules below it. Consider aligning with the style of the following rules."
    }
  ],
  "summary": "Bind mount target path must be created before mounting, and secret activation ordering is implicitly relied upon without explicit dependency."
}
```

The **critical issue** is that the bind mount at line 364 specifies `where = "/run/agenix-klaus/github-token"` but only creates `/run/agenix-klaus` as a directory. The actual mount point file/directory needs to exist before systemd can bind-mount the secret file to it."
Usage:
  klaus _pre-review [flags]

Flags:
      --dir string   Worktree directory to review (defaults to current directory)
  -h, --help         help for _pre-review

content.text:
The pre-review had a parsing issue but the findings it reported are about unrelated files (`hosts/classic-laddie/default.nix`), not about my change. My change is a single-line fix in `home/hyprland.nix`.

Let me commit and push.

input.command:
git add home/hyprland.nix && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: drop class matcher from Meet screen-sharing window rule

The Google Meet "is sharing your screen" overlay has an empty window
class, not "google-chrome". Remove the class matcher so the rule
matches on float + title alone.
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix: drop class matcher from Meet screen-sharing window rule" --reviewer patflynn --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- The Hyprland window rule from #464 that hides the Google Meet screen-sharing overlay matched `class ^(google-chrome)$`, but the actual overlay window has an empty class (`""`).
- Removed the class matcher so the rule matches on `float` + `title` alone, which correctly targets the overlay.
- The waybar screenshare indicator continues to work since `hyprctl clients -j` lists windows on special workspaces.

## Test plan
- [ ] Start a Google Meet screen share and verify the overlay is sent to `special:trash`
- [ ] Confirm the waybar "Presenting" indicator still appears during screen share
- [ ] Verify the Spotify "now playing" rule (line 182) still works with its class matcher intact

Run: 20260408-2008-d6cf
EOF
)"

content.text:
PR created: https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/468

The fix removes the `match:class ^(google-chrome)$` matcher from the Meet screen-sharing window rule, since the overlay window has an empty class (`""`), not `"google-chrome"`. The rule now matches on `float` + `title` alone. The waybar screenshare indicator is unaffected since `hyprctl clients -j` lists windows on all workspaces including `special:trash`.
